title: Pack manifest (on-disk form)
description: Schema for one pack.yaml file under packs/<pack_id>/. A pack is a high-level concern bundle (security, quality, reliability, brand) that the orchestrator engages at step 0. It carries a thin manifest, an interview prompt the orchestrator runs against the user (skipping questions already answered by step-0 product analysis), and a failure-synthesis prompt invoked at step 4. The validator enforces this schema when the bundle is loaded; SKILL.md describes how the orchestrator uses each field.

id.description: Stable pack identifier. Single noun, kebab- or snake-case, max 32 chars. Used in failure_modes[].pack_ids.

version.description: Semantic version of the pack content.

description.description: One-sentence summary of the pack's stance.

tier_hint.description: Informational hint for the consuming product's tier gating. The orchestrator and validator never enforce this — your product does.

interview_prompt.description: Path (relative to the pack directory) of the markdown file containing the interview questions. Each question must declare which step-0 fields can pre-fill it so the orchestrator skips questions already answered by code analysis.

failure_prompt.description: Path (relative to the pack directory) of the markdown file containing the failure-synthesis prompt. Invoked at step 4 after the interview is complete.

applies_when.description: When the pack auto-recommends itself during step 0. Informational — the orchestrator surfaces matching packs as recommended-on-by-default; the user can still toggle.

auto_signals.description: Human-readable conditions checked against the step-0 artifacts. E.g. 'product_profile.data_sensitivity is non-empty'.

default_layer_distribution.description: Soft target for how the pack's contributions distribute across failure layers. Audit at step 4.7 uses this to surface under-coverage.

contributes_compliance_tags.description: Control IDs the pack's failures may carry (static set). The runtime set on any one failure is a subset chosen by the interview answers — e.g. only HIPAA tags appear if the user answered 'HIPAA' to the regulations question.

dependencies.description: Other pack ids this pack assumes are loaded. Validator warns if a dependency is missing.

conflicts.description: Pack ids this pack cannot coexist with. Validator errors if a conflict is loaded simultaneously.
